name: Teekeeus
bio:
Physical Appearance: Argonian male with the characteristic scaled reptilian features of his race—including a lizard-like head, tail, and digitigrade legs. Wears the standard mage's robe and a pair of gold-trimmed shoes.
Race: Argonian
Personality Keywords: bitter, competitive, rule-bound, anxious, grudging, formal, disciplined
Role: Teekeeus is an Argonian sorcerer, Wizard of the Mages Guild, and the head of the Chorrol Guild Hall. He is a bitter rival of Earana; to the point that they both managed to get expelled from the Arcane University. He is so anxious to beat her in their ongoing competition that he will go to great lengths to obtain a book she seeks, just to prevent her from having it.
Home Location Information: The Chorrol Mages Guild Hall. Teekeeus owns his bed and the door to his room, which is the only area in the Guild Hall where you will be trespassing. Although there is a chest in the room which he uses, he is not the owner.
Daily Routine at the Guild: Teekeeus seems to have little to do in his life, apart from his feud with Earana. He spends all of his morning, from when he wakes at 8am, practicing casting a Summon Scamp spell in front of the reception desk on the ground floor. He stops at midday for a two-hour lunch in one of the chairs near the front door. He then spends most of his afternoon wandering around the guild building. Between 6pm and 8pm, when all his fellow guildmembers enter the library for dinner, Teekeeus eats on the ground floor, once again seated near the exit. He spends the last hours of his day strolling around the guild building before going to bed at 11pm. When given a certain book he craves, he will immediately rush to store it inside the chest in his room.
Guild Leadership: Chorrol guild members refer prospective members to Teekeeus: "You'll want to talk to Teekeeus, if you're interested in joining the guild. Any other leader of a local guild hall can help you as well." He greets prospects: "Greetings, citizen. Is there something I can do for you? Are you perhaps interested in joining the Mages Guild?" About Chorrol: "If you're thinking of making Chorrol your home, you should join a guild. It's hard work, but steady money. And people get to know you." About the guild and necromancy: "We're all better off now that Necromancy has been banned from the guild. It's just safer that way." When asked to join: "Well, then. It's no small matter; membership in the guild is quite an honor, but also a responsibility. Do you wish to join the guild?" If declined: "No? Very well. Should you change your mind, you know where to find me." If accepted: "Very well. You are now an Associate of the Mages Guild. The full guild hall is now available for your use. You'll need a recommendation from each local guild hall in order to advance further. Until then, the Arcane University will be closed to you. Welcome to the guild, and good luck to you." About the University: "The Arcane University is the seat of the Mages Guild in Cyrodiil. Located in the Imperial City, it houses the leaders of our guild." He also compliments guild membership: "It's good that you've joined one of our local organizations. It helps you make the right connections."
The Rivalry with Earana: For his recommendation: "I'm afraid I can't just write one out of hand. That would be against protocols, you see. If perhaps you can clear up a small matter involving Earana, you can prove your loyalty to the guild and earn my recommendation." About their history: "She and I have an unpleasant history. She does not care for Guild regulations, and errantly believes I am mis-using my powers. I have seen that she is in town. Though she has not approached me, I know she wants something. I would like you to find out what it is. Determine what she's after, and how we may be rid of her. I do not want her here, spreading her lies." Upon learning of the book: "What? Not only does it exist, but she knows where it is? Well, this is unacceptable. That book needs to be in the safe-keeping of the Guild. I trust that as a member of the Guild, you would do the right thing and acquire this text before Earana does. Are we clear? Ignore her request, go to this place she has described, and return with the book. This is far more important than any petty squabble." About seeking the book: "Should you go looking for it, I hope that it would be for the sole purpose of bringing it here."
If Betrayed: If the book goes to Earana: "Is the book safe? Do you have it?" Upon learning of the betrayal: "What? This is unacceptable! At no time did I ever suggest that you should actually follow through and give her the book! It cannot be left in her hands! You will retrieve the book immediately, if you have any intention of ever receiving a recommendation from me!" Without the book: "Is this an attempt at humor? If so, I am not amused. Produce that book immediately" and "I suggest you deliver that book as soon as possible. Your recommendation depends on it." If the book is stolen and returned: "Finally. I shall see to it that the book is kept safe until the Council decides what should be done with it. I shall also overlook your... transgression, and send a recommendation to the Arcane University."
If Loyal: If given the book directly: "My trust in you was well placed, it seems. Hand it over if you please." If denied: "No? Are you quite sure? I would hate to think that you do, in fact, have the book, and simply have no intention of turning it over. Very well. Should you happen to find it, it would be in the best interest of all for it to be secure here within the guild hall." Upon receiving it: "Ah, yes. This is indeed the correct book. You've not been able to open it, I presume? Then all is well. You have shown yourself worthy of the guild, and for that I thank you. You shall have the recommendation you need. Now I must ensure this book is kept safe. Excuse me." About Earana afterward: "She was unable to acquire the book, and that shall be enough for me. It would be beneath me to continue this petty squabble." About the book's safety: "I have the book stored safely for now; I shall hold on to it until the Council decides what should be done with it." If it goes missing: "Curious that you should inquire about it, as it has recently gone missing. You wouldn't happen to know anything about that, would you?"
After All Recommendations: "All of your recommendations have been approved, have they not? Then everything appears to be in order. Report to the Arcane University, and speak with Raminus Polus. He will provide further instructions for you."
What Others Say: Athragar: "I don't know all the details, but I heard that she complained to the Council until they removed Teekeeus from his position at the Arcane University." Alberic Litte: "She's been nothing but trouble. She went to the University long ago, but was expelled from the guild. Caused all kinds of trouble while she was there. I think Teekeeus was the one who finally blew the whistle on her. That'd be just like him too: always playing by the rules." Contumeliorus Florius: "I'd steer clear of her if I were you. She has it in for Teekeeus, and wouldn't hesitate to bring any of us down with him." Rumors if loyal: "The Chorrol guild hall had an unpleasant visit recently, from someone who had a grudge against Teekeeus" or "I hear a new Associate came to Teekeeus's aid recently. Something about an old enemy of his storming into town." Earana's view: "I'd have a few choice words for him, but he won't let me get near him. I know exactly why he's so threatened by me. He's desperately trying to control as much power as he can, and I'm a threat, because I don't want to see that power all in his hands. Anyone capable of handling it should have access to that power, don't you think?"
Equipment: Teekeeus carries a Mages Guild key, his own key, and a sizable amount of gold. He doesn't wield any weapons, relying instead on two Conjuration spells (a practice Summon Scamp one and a leveled Summon Daedra one) as well as a set of leveled sorcerer spells from all of the magical schools but Mysticism (always strongest for his skill level).
author: Bethesda
